subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, MCC, Freedom House, RSF, PreventionWeb) + international press.
Among Africa's best-governed democracies (top-tier Freedom House 'Free', repeated peaceful alternations, free press), with a tourism-and-remittance economy on a euro-pegged escudo.

Standout features: strong institutions and a credible disinflation, against high public debt (~105% of GDP, declining under an IMF ECF/RSF), deep European-tourism dependence, and an arid, water-scarce archipelago facing chronic drought and rare Fogo volcanism.

economic.notes: A peg-anchored, low-inflation tourism economy with high-but-declining debt.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt high at ~105% of GDP but on a firm downward path under an on-track IMF ECF; still elevated for a small open economy. (IMF May-2026)

monetary_independence.notes: The escudo is hard-pegged to the euro, backed by a Portuguese Treasury credit line - stability bought with near-zero autonomy. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The escudo is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Low and peg-anchored (~2.3%), tracking the euro area - a structural strength. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Broadly stable and supervised within the euro-peg framework, with some legacy NPLs. (IMF; Coface)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Heavily concentrated in tourism/services and reliant on imported food/fuel - highly exposed to European demand. (IMF; AfDB)

institutional.notes: Strong-for-region rule of law, credible data, a capable state, and a free press.

rule_of_law.notes: Strong by African standards - independent courts and respected institutions, though an overburdened court system slows justice. (Freedom House 2025; MCC)

statistical_integrity.notes: Credible, IMF/World Bank-engaged statistics with frequent reviews. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A comparatively capable, technocratic administration that has executed sustained reform. (IMF/World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: Free and pluralistic media, among the best in Africa. (RSF; Freedom House 2025)

political_social.notes: A consolidated democracy with high social trust, thinned over time by emigration.

political_stability.notes: Decades of competitive elections and peaceful transfers, with a 2026 general election approaching and no instability signals. (Freedom House 2025)

civil_liberties.notes: Broadly protected speech/assembly/association, with corruption allegations and judicial backlog the main caveats. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: No systematic abuse - a tourism economy generally open to foreigners and migrants. (observed)

social_cohesion.notes: A homogeneous Creole society with high social trust and low political violence. (Freedom House)

demographics.notes: Heavy emigration and a large diaspora sustain remittances but thin the domestic labour force over time. (World Bank/UN)

geopolitical.notes: A reliable Western/EU/US partner, conflict-free and unsanctioned.

alliance_reliability.notes: A reliable Western/EU/US partner with a special EU partnership and an MCC compact track record. (MCC)

conflict_involvement.notes: No conflict involvement - mid-Atlantic islands far from regional insurgencies. (observed)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Unsanctioned and open under an IMF program; the euro peg implies orthodox policy. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Good regional infrastructure and relative safety, against chronic drought and rare volcanism.

climate.notes: A Sahelian-Atlantic island state - chronic drought/water scarcity plus sea-level/coastal exposure, with relatively higher SIDS readiness. (ND-GAIN; IPCC)

healthcare.notes: A functional basic public system but limited, with advanced care concentrated in Praia/Mindelo and supply shortages. (OSAC)

infrastructure.notes: Relatively good for the region (airports, ports, telecom) but reliant on imported thermal power and costly inter-island logistics. (AfDB)

crime_safety.notes: One of Africa's safer countries, though rising petty/violent crime in Praia prompted a Dec-2025 US advisory note. (OSAC)

disaster_insurance.notes: Thin penetration against drought and rare Fogo volcanic risk. (World Bank; PreventionWeb)

summary: A well-governed outlier - among Africa's strongest democracies (top-tier Freedom House 'Free', repeated peaceful alternations, free press, a capable technocratic state) with a tourism-and-remittance, services-led economy that grew ~6.3% in 2025 on record arrivals, with ~2.3% euro-peg-anchored inflation. Its escudo is hard-pegged to the euro under a Portuguese Treasury credit line, trading monetary autonomy for stability; public debt is high (~105% of GDP) but declining on a firm path under an on-track IMF ECF/RSF program. The structural risks are economic and physical rather than political: deep dependence on European tourism and imported food/fuel, and an arid, water-scarce archipelago facing chronic drought (managed via costly desalination) plus rare Fogo volcanic hazard.
